methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Dusty Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Tessa Lau that has raised $70M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of nine comparable companies—one public and eight private—across the robotics sector at the same stage, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $70M total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $133M. Peer-median valuation across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $1.1B, based on a range from $300M (Fairmat, Collaborative Robotics floor) to $10.0B (1X Technologies ceiling). The peer median reflects substantial variation in market valuations within the robotics sector at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $113M to $1.2B, with a median estimate of $604M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty: the peer set exhibits a 33x spread between minimum and maximum, suggesting that stage-progression alone undervalues relative to recent sector comparables, while the peer median may not fully account for Dusty Robotics' specific traction, market position, or capital efficiency relative to the sample. Key drivers of final valuation include relative progress on commercialization, competitive differentiation within manufacturing automation, and burn rate relative to raised capital.
